Objective: Verify GPIO negative-edge interrupt functionality for pads 8..39: enable per-pin interrupt, generate a falling edge, service ISR, and validate group status and clear paths.
Steps: 1) Initialize test_err=0. 2) Conditionally enable GIC IRQ 87 (GPIO0) or 88 (GPIO1) based on build macro. 3) Enable corresponding system interrupt via MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 and LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO{0/1}_INTR. 4) Drive 0xA0243ffc=0xFFFFFFFF to set all pads high. 5) For i=0..31: configure pad (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4) with (BIT20|BIT18|BIT16); wait_on(10) between pads. 6) For i=0..31 (per-pin test): a) wr_val=1<<i. b) Clear raw status: write MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1=wr_val. c) Enable group1 per-pin interrupt: write MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1=wr_val; wait_on(10). d) Set int_pend=1. e) Pre-charge high: write 0xA0243ffc=0xFFFFFFFF; wait_on(30). f) Create negedge on target pad: write 0xA0243ffc=~wr_val. g) Poll for ISR completion: while(int_pend && timeout--){wait_on(10)} with timeout=5000. h) If timeout==0: log error and increment test_err. 7) End: finish(test_err).
ExpectedResult: ISR is invoked for each targeted pad; within ISR: pad status bit[0]==0 (no posedge), edge status bit[1]==1 (negedge detected); group status has the target bit set on entry, becomes 0 after clearing raw status; GIC/SYSREG status cleared. No timeouts. finish(test_err)==0.
PassCriteria: - No timeout occurs during ISR wait. - In ISR: (rdata & 0x1)==0; (rdata & 0x2)!=0. - (read MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 & (1<<i))!=0 on entry, becomes 0 after clears. - final test_err==0.
Dependencies: test_common.h APIs (read_reg, write_reg, wait_on, finish), lss_sysreg.h macros, gpio/gpio_def.h, gpio/gpio_offset.h; Makefile includes $(VERIF)/C_ENV/SOC_ENV/make.include.c_env
Notes: Explicit addresses used: 0xA0243ffc. Conditional build macros GPIO0/GPIO1 select IRQ lines and SYSREG bits. int_pend is polled with timeout=5000 and wait_on(10).}]}
